evals.prompt: I just open-sourced a Python CLI tool called 'taskflow' that helps manage task pipelines. The repo has a basic README and a LICENSE but nothing else. Can you make it look professional? Here's what it does: it reads YAML task definitions and runs them in dependency order with parallel execution. Main audience is DevOps engineers. We use pytest for testing and ruff for linting.
evals.expected_behavior: Recognizes this as a repository-quality and health-file task, audits the current repo state, and recommends README plus community-file improvements tailored to a Python CLI for DevOps engineers.
assertions: Routes to github-repo rather than github-profile
Identifies missing README-adjacent and community-health surfaces
Tailors recommendations to a Python CLI repository

evals.prompt: We're setting up our company's GitHub org (called 'acme-dev'). We need default community health files, issue templates, and a nice org profile page. We build developer tools — our main products are an API gateway and a monitoring SDK. We have about 15 public repos.
evals.expected_behavior: Handles this as an organization-wide repository-health setup task, recommends org-level .github defaults plus profile surfaces, and scopes the work to reusable repository standards.
assertions: Includes community health files and template recommendations
Recognizes that org profile work can be part of a broader repository-quality setup

evals.prompt: Review my repo and tell me what I'm missing. It's a React component library called 'ui-primitives'. We have a README with install instructions and a basic API reference, a LICENSE (MIT), and a CI workflow. No other community files or templates.
evals.expected_behavior: Produces a repository-quality audit focused on missing community files, templates, metadata, and polish needed for a React component library.
assertions: Performs an audit rather than only generating boilerplate
Identifies missing community files and issue or PR template surfaces
Tailors the guidance to a React component library
